plugins.name: Snapshot on Event
plugins.description: Captures a high-quality still image whenever an event occurs.
plugins.value: Often more useful than full video clips for quick inspection.
plugins.comments: Extremely practical and lightweight. Strong complement to event recording.

plugins.name: Retention Policy Manager
plugins.description: Automatically deletes old clips, snapshots, or timelapses based on age or storage limits.
plugins.value: Prevents storage from filling up and keeps the system stable over time.
plugins.comments: Extremely practical and essential for real-world usage. Strong local-first alignment and very easy for users to understand.

plugins.name: Snapshot Capture
plugins.description: Captures still images on interval, manual trigger, or event.
plugins.value: Simple and versatile capture method for many use cases.
plugins.comments: Highly flexible and useful across all scenarios including pets, printers, and workshops.

plugins.name: Motion Detection (Basic)
plugins.description: Detects motion using frame differencing.
plugins.value: Essential for security and automation use cases.
plugins.comments: Excellent first-party plugin candidate. Strong fit for hobbyist monitoring, local-first use, and single-container Raspberry Pi deployment. Although marked as core, this is a very good plugin-shaped capability.

plugins.name: Disk Space Guard
plugins.description: Monitors disk usage and warns or pauses recording when storage becomes critically low.
plugins.value: Prevents crashes and data loss due to full storage.
plugins.comments: Highly practical safeguard. Complements recording plugins and prevents common real-world failure scenarios.

plugins.name: Thermal Watchdog
plugins.description: Monitors Raspberry Pi temperature and warns about thermal throttling.
plugins.value: Helps maintain system performance and reliability.
plugins.comments: Highly relevant for Raspberry Pi users. Very low risk and strong practical benefit.

plugins.name: Metrics Dashboard
plugins.description: Displays FPS, latency, CPU usage, and stream diagnostics.
plugins.comments: Excellent fit for tinkerers, makers, and developers. Strong observability value with very good plugin boundaries and low trust risk.

plugins.name: Timelapse Generator
plugins.description: Creates timelapse videos from captured frames.
plugins.value: Great for hobby and creative use.
plugins.comments: One of the best first-party plugin ideas in the whole set. Great fit for makers, 3D printing, workshop monitoring, and creative hobby usage.

plugins.name: Event-Based Recording
plugins.description: Records clips when motion or events are detected.
plugins.comments: Excellent fit for your audience and product shape. Highly practical, resource-aware, and aligned with local-first monitoring use cases.

plugins.name: PIR Assist
plugins.description: Combines GPIO PIR sensor input with motion detection to reduce false positives.
plugins.value: Improves detection accuracy using physical sensors.
plugins.comments: Excellent maker-focused plugin. Strong real-world accuracy improvements using cheap hardware.

plugins.name: GPIO Trigger
plugins.description: Triggers events based on GPIO input (e.g. PIR sensor).
plugins.comments: Excellent match for Raspberry Pi tinkerers and makers. Strong differentiation point and very aligned with the physical-computing audience.

plugins.name: MQTT Publisher
plugins.description: Publishes events to an MQTT broker for home automation systems.
plugins.value: Enables integration with Home Assistant and other automation platforms.
plugins.comments: One of the strongest ecosystem integrations for your audience. Perfect for tinkerers and home automation setups.

plugins.name: Webhook Notifications
plugins.description: Sends event data to external systems via webhooks.
plugins.comments: One of the strongest integration plugins in the list. Very extensible and works well for local automations, LAN tooling, and advanced hobbyist setups.

plugins.name: GIF Creator
plugins.description: Generates GIFs from clips or events.
plugins.comments: A fun but still practical output plugin. Good optional feature that is easy to understand and fits well inside a clean plugin boundary.

plugins.name: Plugin Health Panel
plugins.description: Displays status and health of all enabled plugins.
plugins.value: Improves visibility and debugging of plugin system.
plugins.comments: Very useful once plugin ecosystem grows. Enhances transparency and trust.

plugins.name: Event Timeline
plugins.description: Displays a chronological timeline of detected events with thumbnails and playback.
plugins.value: Makes reviewing footage intuitive and efficient.
plugins.comments: Transforms raw detections into usable insight. Strong UX improvement for all users.

plugins.name: SMB / NAS Export
plugins.description: Saves recordings and snapshots to a local network share.
plugins.value: Provides local storage expansion without cloud dependency.
plugins.comments: Strong alternative to cloud storage. Keeps the system aligned with local-first principles.

plugins.name: Edge Detection Filter
plugins.description: Applies edge detection to the video stream.
plugins.value: Useful for debugging and creative use cases.
plugins.comments: Not essential, but a very clean plugin idea for debugging, experimentation, and creative hobby use. Low risk and easy to keep optional.

plugins.name: Basic Authentication
plugins.description: Protects streams with username/password.
plugins.comments: Important from a trust perspective. It works as a plugin technically, but there is also a strong case for including some basic protection in core.

plugins.name: Motion Detection (Advanced)
plugins.description: Background subtraction and noise filtering for more accurate motion detection.
plugins.comments: Good second-step plugin after basic motion detection. Clear user value and good local-first fit, but slightly more algorithmic complexity and tuning burden.

plugins.name: Motion Zones
plugins.description: Allows defining specific areas of the frame for motion detection.
plugins.comments: Very good practical plugin for workshops, printers, benches, and doorways. Strong control benefit with limited trust or deployment concerns.

plugins.name: Clip Buffer
plugins.description: Maintains a rolling buffer to capture moments before an event.
plugins.comments: Very practical for real monitoring scenarios. Slightly more operational complexity than basic event recording, but strong value and still a good plugin fit.

plugins.name: Scheduler
plugins.description: Schedules when plugins or features are active.
plugins.comments: Very practical and easy to justify for workshops, pet monitoring, and time-bound monitoring scenarios. Good balance of usefulness and manageable complexity.

plugins.name: Continuous Recording
plugins.description: Records the stream continuously.
plugins.comments: Clear and useful, but can be storage- and resource-heavy for Pi users. Good plugin candidate rather than a mandatory built-in feature.

plugins.name: Telegram Notifications
plugins.description: Sends alerts via Telegram.
plugins.comments: Useful and easy to explain, but weaker fit for a local-first system because it depends on an external service and raises privacy and trust expectations.

plugins.name: HLS Streaming
plugins.description: Outputs the stream in HLS format.
plugins.comments: Practical and web-friendly, though less aligned with the simplest local preview story. A good extension, but not one of the very strongest early plugins.

plugins.name: Night Mode Filter
plugins.description: Enhances low-light video conditions.
plugins.comments: Useful for garages, workshops, and pet monitoring, but video enhancement can become hardware-sensitive. Still a solid plugin candidate.

plugins.name: Cloudflare R2 Upload
plugins.description: Uploads recordings to Cloudflare R2 storage.
plugins.comments: Useful for users who want off-device storage, but it is less aligned with local-first reliability and introduces credential and upload-trust concerns.

plugins.name: Mio Reactive UI
plugins.description: Animates the mascot based on system state (idle, motion, alert).
plugins.comments: A nice brand and delight feature, but not especially strong on practical value. Good optional plugin rather than something to prioritise early.

plugins.name: Rules Engine
plugins.description: Allows combining conditions and actions (e.g. motion + person → notify).
plugins.comments: Potentially platform-defining, but easy to overcomplicate. High value, yet needs very disciplined UX and scope control to avoid becoming a maintenance trap.

plugins.name: Person Detection
plugins.description: Detects human presence using lightweight AI models.
plugins.value: Adds intelligence beyond basic motion detection.
plugins.comments: Interesting and valuable, but AI inference on Pi 4 raises reliability and maintenance concerns. Worth considering later, especially if model scope is tightly constrained.

plugins.name: WebRTC Streaming
plugins.description: Low-latency real-time streaming using WebRTC.
plugins.comments: Attractive on paper, but too complex relative to your single-container and easy-first-run goals. High implementation and support burden make this a later-phase idea.

plugins.name: Animal Detection
plugins.description: Detects animals (pets/wildlife) in the frame.
plugins.value: Useful for outdoor and hobby use cases.
plugins.comments: Useful for a narrower audience, but lower return than person detection and similar AI-related complexity risk. Better as a later plugin, not an early first-party investment.

plugins.name: Scene Description
plugins.description: Generates natural language descriptions of events in the scene.
plugins.verdict: reject - not fit for early phase
plugins.comments: Interesting idea, but a poor fit for your current local-first, Pi 4, single-container goals. High compute cost and likely internet dependency make it a bad early first-party plugin.
